DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SANCTIONS AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOVED FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT TO EXCLUDE ANY FUTURE TESTIMONY BY PATRICK NELSON, A PRINCIPAL OF DEFENDANT. A JANUARY 17, 2014 COURT ORDER DIRECTED DEFENDANT TO PRODUCE FOR DEPOSITION A "WITNESS ... PURSUANT TO PLAINTIFF'S SEPTEMBER 10, 2013 DEPOSITION NOTICE." PLAINTIFF ARGUES THAT THE ORDER, STIPULATION AND THE LAW REQUIRED THAT DEFENDANT PRODUCE AS A DEPOSITION WITNESS A PERSON "MOST QUALIFIED TO TESTIFY". DEFENDANT PRODUCED BEAU JAUSSI, AN APPARENTLY WELL PREPARED, QUALIFIED AND RELATIVELY SENIOR REPRESENTATIVE OF DEFENDANT, WHO APPARENTLY WAS PARTY TO, OR COPIED ON, MUCH OF THE CORRESPONDENCE, ELECTRONIC OR OTHERWISE, IN WHICH PATRICK NELSON WAS INVOLVED, OR TO WHICH HE WAS PRIVY, RELATING TO THE SALES TRANSACTION AT ISSUE IN THE CASE. HE ALSO HAD BEEN INTIMATELY INVOLVED IN THE SALES TRANSACTION. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION OF MR. JAUSSI LASTED APPROXIMATELY 7 HOURS. TOWARD THE END OF THE DEPOSITION, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL OBJECTED TO MR. JAUSSI AS A DEPOSITION WITNESS AND SAID HE BELIEVED THAT PATRICK NELSON SHOULD BE PRODUCED AS THE WITNESS IN RESPONSE TO THE NOTICE OF DEPOSITION. WHILE DISAGREEING AT THE DEPOSITION THAT MR. NELSON SHOULD HAVE BEEN THE WITNESS THAT DAY, COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT NEVERTHELESS OFFERED TO PRODUCE MR. NELSON AS A WITNESS AT A DATE AGREEABLE TO THE PARTIES AND FOLLOWED UP THAT OFFER THE NEXT DAY. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT LATER ASKED FOR A MEET AND CONFER ABOUT AVAILABILITY WHEN PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL QUESTIONED WHETHER DEFENDANT WOULD ACTUALLY PRODUCE MR. NELSON AT A DEPOSITION. INSTEAD OF MAKING ARRANGEMENTS TO DEPOSE MR. NELSON OR EVEN DISCUSSING THE MATTER ABOUT AVAILABLE DATES, PLAINTIFF BROUGHT THE INSTANT MOTION FOR SANCTIONS. IN VIEW OF THE FOREGOING CIRCUMSTANCES, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Charles M. Schaible, Esq., Cooley LLP, 415.693.2101, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Matt Evans, Esq., appearing by Court Call for the Defendants.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling regarding PLAINTIFF 851 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions as follows: Plaintiff's moved for evidentiary sanctions against defendant to exclude any future testimony by Patrick Nelson, a principal of defendant. A January 17, 2014 court order directed defendant to produce for deposition a "witness ... pursuant to plaintiff's September 10, 2013 deposition notice." Plaintiff argues that the order, stipulation and the law required that defendant produce as a deposition witness a person "most qualified to testify". Defendant produced Beau Jaussi, an apparently well prepared, qualified and relatively senior representative of defendant, who apparently was party to, or copied on, much of the correspondence, electronic or otherwise, in which Patrick Nelson was involved, or to which he was privy, relating to the sales transaction at issue in the case. He also had been intimately involved in the sales transaction. Plaintiff's deposition of Mr. Jaussi lasted approximately 7 hours. Toward the end of the deposition, plaintiff's counsel objected to Mr. Jaussi as a deposition witness and said he believed that Patrick Nelson should be produced as the witness in response to the Notice of Deposition. While disagreeing at the deposition that Mr. Nelson should have been the witness that day, counsel for defendant nevertheless offered to produce Mr. Nelson as a witness at a date agreeable to the parties and followed up that offer the next day. Counsel for defendant later asked for a meet and confer about availability when plaintiff's counsel questioned whether defendant would actually produce Mr. Nelson at a deposition. Instead of making arrangements to depose Mr. Nelson or even discussing the matter about available dates, plaintiff brought the instant motion for sanctions. In view of the foregoing circumstances, plaintiff's motion for evidentiary sanctions is denied. Mr. Schiable stated that opposing counsel's proposed order did not conform with the tentative ruling. The Court directed Mr. Evans to prepare a proposed order that conforms with the Court's tentative ruling, send a copy to Mr. Schaible, and then submit the new order to the Court. Judge Pro Tem: Adrian A. Colley; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. =302/JPT
